title: Intensely Earthy Syrian Avocado Tagine
description: A Syrian-inspired, earthy, and velvety dish featuring avocado, perfect for a festive occasion. This recipe combines rich flavors and textures with the added depth of binchotan charcoal.

ingredients.name: Avocado
ingredients.notes: Ripe avocados are essential for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Binchotan charcoal
ingredients.notes: Binchotan charcoal adds a rich, earthy flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Pomegranate molasses
ingredients.notes: This sweet and tangy ingredient is a staple in Syrian cuisine.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped fresh parsley adds a pop of color and freshness to the dish.

instructions: Heat binchotan charcoal in a charcoal chimney until it's lit and smoldering.
In a large clay or ceramic pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it's translucent and slightly caramelized.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the diced avocado and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it starts to soften.
Add the pomegranate molasses, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the flavors have melded together.
Serve the Intensely Earthy Syrian Avocado Tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let the dish rest for 10-15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
This tagine can be made ahead of time and refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
To reheat, simply place the dish over low heat and stir in some additional olive oil if needed.
This recipe is best served with a side of crusty bread or over couscous.
This dish is naturally gluten-free, making it a great option for those with gluten intolerance or sensitivity.
